name: null
description: end each output line with NUL, not newline

name: all
description: write counts for all files, not just directories

name: apparent-size
description: print apparent sizes rather than device usage; although the apparent size is usually smaller, it may be larger due to holes in ('sparse') files, internal fragmentation, indirect blocks, etc

name: block-size
description: scale sizes by SIZE before printing them; See SIZE format below; E.g., '-BM' prints sizes in units of 1,048,576 bytes

name: bytes
description: equivalent to '--apparent-size --block-size=1'

name: total
description: produce a grand total

name: dereference-args
description: dereference only symlinks that are listed on the command line

name: max-depth
description: print the total for a directory (or file, with --all) only if it is N or fewer levels below the command line argument; --max-depth=0 is the same as --summarize --files0-from=F summarize device usage of the NUL-terminated file names specified in file F; if F is -, read names from standard input

name: flag-H
description: equivalent to --dereference-args (-D)

name: human-readable
description: print sizes in human readable format (e.g., 1K 234M 2G) --inodes list inode usage information instead of block usage

name: flag-k
description: like --block-size=1K

name: dereference
description: dereference all symbolic links

name: count-links
description: count sizes many times if hard linked

name: flag-m
description: like --block-size=1M

name: no-dereference
description: don't follow any symbolic links (this is the default)

name: separate-dirs
description: for directories do not include size of subdirectories --si like -h, but use powers of 1000 not 1024

name: summarize
description: display only a total for each argument

name: threshold
description: exclude entries smaller than SIZE if positive, or entries greater than SIZE if negative --time show time of the last modification of any file in the directory, or any of its subdirectories --time=WORD show time as WORD instead of modification time: atime, access, use, ctime or status --time-style=STYLE time/date format with --time; see TIME_STYLE below

name: exclude-from
description: exclude files that match any pattern in FILE --exclude=PATTERN exclude files that match PATTERN

name: one-file-system
description: skip directories on different file systems --help display this help and exit --version output version information and exit
